div1.entryFree: Tread, subst. footing, stepping with the foot: her feet were much too dainty for such t. LLL IV, 3, 279. the quaint mazes . . . for lack of t. are undistinguishable, Mids. II, 1, 100. it no more merits the t. of a man's foot, All's II, 3, 292. list if thou canst hear the t. of travellers, H4A II, 2, 35.
